narrative_blocks.header: Died at Rushville Illinois eighteen months after marriage
narrative_blocks.body: Married Dr Joseph Washburn Clark of Rushville IL on her twenty-fifth birthday September 28, 1837 and moved with him to the Illinois frontier. Eighteen months later — five weeks after the April 12 birth of their only child William Hooker Clark — she died at Rushville on April 17, 1839. The infant followed her on May 6, 1839.

narrative_blocks.header: Died at Rushville Illinois eighteen months after marriage
narrative_blocks.body: Married Dr Joseph Washburn Clark of Rushville IL on her twenty-fifth birthday September 28, 1837 and moved with him to the Illinois frontier. Eighteen months later — five weeks after the April 12 birth of their only child William Hooker Clark — she died at Rushville on April 17, 1839. The infant followed her on May 6, 1839.

death.notes: Likely died San Francisco area given final burial relocation.

burial.plot_notes: Originally interred at Lone Mountain Cemetery San Francisco; removed to Cypress Lawn Memorial Park Colma when San Francisco relocated in-city cemeteries to San Mateo County.

narrative_blocks.header: Lost first family Illinois, finally buried California
narrative_blocks.body: Lost his young wife Lucy Ashmun Hooker in April 1839 at Rushville IL and their infant son five weeks later in May. Survived them by 39 years; eventually died 1878 with California burial — interred at San Francisco's Lone Mountain Cemetery, then later removed to Cypress Lawn Memorial Park Colma when San Francisco relocated its in-city cemeteries to San Mateo County.

death.notes: Died at age 24 days, three weeks after his mother's death.

narrative_blocks.header: Mother died five days after his birth, 1839
narrative_blocks.body: William Hooker Clark was born April 12, 1839 at Rushville Illinois — but his mother Lucy died five days later on April 17, leaving him in the care of his father Dr. Joseph Washburn Clark. He died May 6, 1839, twenty-four days old, never knowing his mother.

parents.father_research_notes: Dr. William Hooker (H00529, 1766-?), physician + trader at West Hampton MA; born Northampton MA Nov 26, 1766; son of Rev John and Sarah Worthington Hooker of Northampton. Per EH 1909 #529: 'William Hooker (John* John* John,3 Samuel,2 Thomas1)...m. 1798 Anna Thompson, daughter of Hugh Thompson of East Windsor CT. She died at West Hampton MA. He married No. 2, Widow of Ensign Hartland.'
parents.mother_research_notes: Anna Thompson Hooker not yet in tree.

career.location: West Hampton, Norwich, and Westfield Massachusetts
career.notes: Merchant at West Hampton MA, then Norwich MA. In 1819 removed family to Westfield MA, where they remained.

narrative_blocks.header: Moved family from Norwich Massachusetts to Westfield, 1819
narrative_blocks.body: Merchant at West Hampton MA before relocating to Norwich MA where his two eldest children Lucy Ashmun and William Throop were born. In 1819 he moved the family to Westfield MA, where son Edward was born 1822 and where William and his wife Octavia would both be buried at Pine Hill Cemetery.

narrative_blocks.header: Daughter Lucy Ashmun died at Rushville Illinois, 1839
narrative_blocks.body: His eldest child Lucy Ashmun died age 26 at Rushville IL April 17, 1839 — only seventeen months after her Massachusetts marriage to Dr Joseph Washburn Clark and five weeks after the birth of their infant son who himself died in May. The Illinois frontier had taken his daughter, son-in-law's first family, and grandson all within a year.

parents.mother_research_notes: Octavia Throop Hale (1754-1839) of Bozrah CT, died Westhampton MA aged 85. Not yet built (deferred).

cross_connections.display_label: Uncle executed as Continental Army spy, September 1776

narrative_blocks.header: Niece of Nathan Hale the patriot martyr
narrative_blocks.body: Her father Rev Enoch Hale of Westhampton MA was elder brother of Capt Nathan Hale — the Continental Army officer hanged as spy by the British at New York September 22, 1776. Nathan died nearly twelve years before her birth; the family memory of the executed uncle shaped her own brother's name (Nathan Hale Sr 1784-1863) and reached into her children's generation through her son's cousin marriage to her sister Sybella Hale Hall's daughter Sarah.

narrative_blocks.header: Died of dementia paralytica at Northampton, 1867
narrative_blocks.body: Died December 21, 1867 at Northampton MA age 79 — eighteen months after her husband — with cause given on her death record as 'Dementile Paralysis,' the period name for dementia paralytica, the late-stage neurological syndrome of progressive cognitive and motor decline.

career.notes: Practiced medicine at Westhampton MA per EH 1909 #529. Established the Hooker physician tradition in Westhampton continued by son Dr. Anson Hooker (H01414, 1799-1869) and through several Westhampton descendants.
Per EH 1909 #529: 'He was a physician and also a trader at West Hampton, Mass.' — supplementing physician income with a trading operation, common pattern for rural New England doctors of the period.

narrative_blocks.header: Lived to ninety-four as Westhampton physician
narrative_blocks.body: Born Northampton MA 1766; died Westhampton MA February 27, 1861 at age ninety-four — extraordinary longevity spanning the early Republic through the Civil War's outbreak. Practiced medicine in Westhampton MA and also operated as a trader; both occupations supported a large family of seven children with first wife Anna Thompson before her 1810 death.

narrative_blocks.header: Lost wife at forty-two, remarried after two years
narrative_blocks.body: First wife Anna Thompson died Westhampton April 2, 1810 at age forty-two — leaving him with seven children ranging from twenty-four-year-old Henry down to eight-year-old Edward. William remarried 1812 to Rhoda Phelps, widow of Ensign Hartland of Hartland Connecticut. No documented children from the second marriage; Rhoda predeceased William by fourteen years.

parents.father_name: Hugh Thompson of East Windsor CT (per EH 1909 #529)
parents.father_research_notes: Per Edward Hooker 1909 #529: 'm. 1798 [sic — likely 1788], Anna Thompson, daughter of Hugh Thompson of East Windsor, Conn.' EH date '1798' may be transcription error; her 1786 first child suggests marriage 1785-86. Hugh Thompson East Windsor specific parentage not yet built.

narrative_blocks.header: Died age forty-two leaving seven children
narrative_blocks.body: Died Westhampton MA April 2, 1810 at age forty-two — leaving seven children ranging from twenty-four-year-old Henry down to eight-year-old Edward. Her son middle-named for her father Hugh Thompson (Hugh Thompson Hooker, born 1789) carries the East Windsor Connecticut Thompson family name forward.

parents.father_research_notes: Deacon Richard Hale (1717-1802) of Coventry CT — paternal grandfather Samuel Hale (1687-1723) of Beverly MA. The Coventry Hales are a separate family from the Wales-Hartford Hales (Samuel Hale Sr 1637) that married into the Talcott line via Sarah Talcott + Jonathan Hale 1717.
parents.mother_research_notes: Elizabeth Strong Hale of Northampton MA, daughter of Joseph Strong Jr (1701-1773) and Elizabeth Strong (1704-1792). Strong family of Northampton.

career.organization: First Congregational Church of West Hampton
career.notes: First minister of West Hampton Massachusetts.

narrative_blocks.header: Brother of Nathan Hale the patriot martyr
narrative_blocks.body: Elder brother of Capt Nathan Hale (1755-1776) — the Continental Army officer hanged by the British at New York September 22, 1776 with the final words 'I only regret that I have but one life to lose for my country.' Enoch survived him by 61 years and shaped his own son's name around the family memory.

narrative_blocks.header: First minister of West Hampton Massachusetts
narrative_blocks.body: Settled at West Hampton MA as the new parish's first minister, where he served the gathered congregation through his long life. Daughter Octavia (1788-1867) born at Westhampton would marry William Hooker Jr there December 19, 1811 and carry the Hale-Hooker connection into the Westfield and Hartford banking families.

burialCemetery.name: Rushville City Cemetery
burialCemetery.notes: Rushville Schuyler County IL. Burial site of Lucy Ashmun Hooker Clark (H02753), who died at Rushville April 17, 1839 age 25. FindAGrave Cemetery #1217216.
